metadata.title: Pain Point Studio
metadata.description: Validate product ideas before writing code. A structured research platform for developers to uncover real user pain points.

descriptions.home: Validate product ideas before writing code. A structured research platform for developers to uncover real user pain points.
descriptions.dashboard: Overview of your research projects, surveys, and recent activity.
descriptions.project: View project details, surveys, and notes.
descriptions.projects: Manage and organize your research projects.
descriptions.projectNew: Create a new research project to start validating your product idea.
descriptions.projectNewSurvey: Create a new survey for your research project.
descriptions.surveyBuilder: Design and build your research survey.
descriptions.surveyStats: View survey responses and analyze research data.
descriptions.settingsProfile: Update your display name, bio, avatar, and social links.
descriptions.settingsConnectedAccounts: Manage linked OAuth providers like Google and GitHub.
descriptions.settingsDangerZone: Delete your account and all associated data.
descriptions.signIn: Sign in to Pain Point Studio to continue your research.
descriptions.signUp: Create a free account and start validating your product ideas.
descriptions.forgotPassword: Reset your Pain Point Studio password.
descriptions.updatePassword: Set a new password for your account.
descriptions.notFound: The page you're looking for doesn't exist or has been moved.

surveys.title: Surveys
surveys.description: Create and manage surveys to collect user feedback.

empty.title: No surveys yet
empty.description: Create a short survey, share the link, and get real feedback from your audience — all in under 15 minutes.

create.title: Survey Details
create.description: Fill in the basic information about your survey.
create.surveyTitlePlaceholder: e.g., Is manual invoicing a real pain for freelancers?
create.surveyDescriptionPlaceholder: What problem are you investigating and what do you want to learn from respondents?
create.surveyDescriptionHelper: For your own reference only. A branded landing page for respondents is coming soon.
create.visibilityPrivateDescription: Only accessible via direct link. Not visible on the platform.
create.visibilityPublicDescription: Accessible via link and visible in the Community module.
create.projectHelper: Link this survey to a research project.

title.title: What's your survey called?
title.hint: Give your survey a clear, descriptive name so you can easily find it later.

description.title: Describe your survey
description.hint: Explain what this survey is about and what you want to learn from respondents.

confirm.title: Review and create
confirm.hint: Check the details below and create your survey. You can always edit them later.

errors.titleTooLong: Title must be 100 characters or fewer.
errors.descriptionTooLong: Description must be 2000 characters or fewer.
errors.unexpected: Something went wrong. Please try again.
errors.conflict: This survey was updated elsewhere. Please refresh and try again.
errors.projectNotActive: The parent project must be active to perform this action.
errors.projectLimitReached: This project has reached its response limit ({limit} responses).
errors.exportTooManyResponses: This survey has too many responses to export at once. Please contact support.
errors.bulkAllFailed: None of the selected surveys could be updated. They may have already changed status.

builder.removeDescriptionConfirm: Are you sure you want to remove the description? This action cannot be undone.
builder.deleteQuestionConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this question? This action cannot be undone.

emptyState.title: Start building your research
emptyState.description: Add your first question to get started.

errors.questionTextTooLong: Question text must be 500 characters or fewer.
errors.descriptionTooLong: Description must be 500 characters or fewer.
errors.optionTooLong: Each option must be 200 characters or fewer.
errors.ratingMinValue: Minimum value must be at least 1.
errors.ratingMaxValue: Maximum value must be at most 10.
errors.ratingMinGreaterMax: Minimum must be less than maximum.
errors.minQuestionsToPublish: Add at least 1 question with text before publishing.
errors.saveFailed: Failed to save questions. Please try again.

publish.settingsDescription: Configure optional settings before publishing your survey.
publish.settingsHint: Both settings are optional. Leave them empty to run without limits.
publish.maxRespondentsHelper: Survey auto-completes after reaching this limit.
publish.successDescription: Share the link below to start collecting responses.
publish.qrDescription: Scan to open the survey on any device.
publish.shareBody:
I'd love your feedback on my survey: {title}

{url}

overview.alertLowCompletion: Completion rate is {rate}% — consider shortening your survey
overview.setupDescription: Complete these steps to start collecting responses.

overviewEmpty.title: No responses yet

questionsEmpty.title: No data yet
questionsEmpty.description: Question analytics will appear once responses are collected.

responseList.selectResponse: Select a response to view details

statusInfo.completed: This response was fully completed by the respondent. All questions were answered and the survey was submitted successfully.
statusInfo.in_progress: This response is currently in progress. The respondent has started the survey but hasn't submitted it yet.
statusInfo.abandoned: This response was abandoned. The respondent started the survey but didn't complete it within the allowed time.

stats.contextBadgeDescription: A survey collects responses from your target audience through structured questions. Each survey belongs to a project and helps gather data to validate or invalidate your assumptions.
stats.completeSurveyConfirm: The survey will stop accepting new responses. The survey link will remain accessible for 14 days, then expire.
stats.cancelSurveyConfirm: This will withdraw the survey. The survey link will remain accessible for 14 days with a withdrawal notice.
stats.exportCSVDescription: Compatible with Excel, Google Sheets, and other spreadsheet apps.
stats.exportJSONDescription: Structured format for developers and data analysis tools.
stats.noResponsesDescription: Share the survey link to start collecting feedback.
stats.responsesCount: {count, plural, =0 {No responses} one {1 response} other {# responses}}
stats.insightText: {count, plural, one {1 response} other {# responses}}, avg. {chars} chars

statusInfo.draft: This survey is still being designed. Add questions and configure settings before publishing.
statusInfo.active: This survey is live and collecting responses from participants.
statusInfo.completed: Data collection is finished. Results are available for review.
statusInfo.trashed: This survey is in the trash and will be permanently deleted in {days} days.

detailPanel.questionsEmptyDescription: This survey has no questions. Add questions in the survey builder to start collecting responses.

confirm.completeDescription: The survey will stop accepting new responses. Respondents currently filling it out will be immediately blocked. The survey link will remain accessible for 14 days showing a completion notice, then expire. All collected responses are preserved.
confirm.trashDescription: The survey will be moved to trash and permanently deleted after 30 days. Respondents currently filling it out will be immediately blocked. All collected responses will be deleted with it. You can restore it from trash before then.
confirm.restoreTrashDescription: The survey will be restored to its previous status.
confirm.permanentDeleteDescription: This will permanently delete the survey, all its questions, and all collected responses. This action cannot be undone.

toast.actionFailed: Something went wrong. Please try again.

table.title: Title

activityInfo.lastEditedDescription: When this draft was last modified. Publishing or changing questions updates this time.
activityInfo.recentActivityDescription: Daily response count for the last 14 days. Shows how many respondents completed the survey each day.
activityInfo.linkExpiresDescription: After completion or cancellation, the survey link stays available for 14 days so respondents can see a notice. Then it expires and the survey is no longer accessible via the link.
activityInfo.linkExpiresInDays: The survey link will expire in {days, plural, one {1 day} other {# days}}.
activityInfo.autoDeletesDescription: Archived surveys are automatically permanently deleted after the retention period. Until then you can restore them.
activityInfo.autoDeletesInDays: This archived survey will be permanently deleted in {days, plural, one {1 day} other {# days}}.
activityInfo.deletedInDaysDescription: This survey is in the trash and will be permanently deleted in {days} days. You can restore it from the menu before then.

projects.title: Projects
projects.description: Manage your research projects and track validation progress.

empty.title: No projects yet
empty.description: Create your first project to organize research around a specific idea or product.
empty.trashedTitle: All projects are in the trash
empty.trashedDescription: Your projects have been moved to trash. Create a new project or restore existing ones from their detail pages.

create.title: New Project
create.pageDescription: Set up a new research project to track your validation journey.
create.name: Project Name
create.summaryHelper: A brief one-liner for what you're researching.
create.summaryPlaceholder: e.g., Validating demand for an AI writing assistant...

name.title: What's your project called?
name.hint: Choose a clear, descriptive name that captures the core idea you're exploring. You can always change it later.

summary.title: Summarize your project
summary.hint: Write a brief one-liner that explains what you're researching or validating. Think of it as an elevator pitch.

description.title: Tell us more about your project
description.hint: Add more context about the problem you're solving, your target audience, or what success looks like. This helps you stay focused as you research.
description.label: Description
description.placeholder: e.g., We're exploring whether freelance designers need a better way to manage client feedback...

confirm.title: Review and create
confirm.hint: Here's what your project will look like. You can go back to make changes, or create it now.

image.title: Add a project image
image.hint: Upload a logo or image to help you quickly identify this project. You can always add or change it later.

table.name: Name
table.deletedInDays: Permanently deleted {days, plural, one {in 1 day} other {in # days}}
table.deletedInDaysShort: {days, plural, one {# day left} other {# days left}}

smartStatus.has_drafts: {count, plural, one {# draft survey} other {# draft surveys}}

description.survey_ending_soon: One of your surveys is about to end soon. Check its progress and decide whether to extend or complete it.
description.no_active_surveys: None of your surveys are currently collecting responses. Activate a draft or create a new survey to continue gathering data.
description.has_drafts: You have draft surveys that haven't been published yet. Review and activate them to start collecting responses.
description.collecting_responses: Your surveys are live and actively collecting responses from participants.
description.no_surveys: This project doesn't have any surveys yet. Create your first survey to start collecting data.
description.all_complete: All surveys in this project have finished collecting responses. Review your findings to draw conclusions.

confirm.completeDescription: This project will be marked as completed. It becomes read-only — reopen it to make changes. Active surveys will be automatically completed, and respondents currently filling them out will be immediately blocked.
confirm.trashDescription: This project will be moved to trash and permanently deleted after 30 days. All surveys in this project will also be moved to trash. You can restore everything from trash before then.
confirm.restoreTrashDescription: This project and all its trashed surveys will be restored to their previous status.
confirm.permanentDeleteDescription: This will permanently delete the project, all its surveys, and all collected responses. This action cannot be undone.

edit.title: Edit Project
edit.description: Update the project name or description.

statusInfo.active: This project is active and accepting new surveys. You can add surveys, collect responses, and track validation progress.
statusInfo.completed: This project's research is complete. It's read-only — reopen it to make changes.
statusInfo.trashed: This project is in the trash and will be permanently deleted in {days} days.

detail.noSurveysInPhase: No surveys in this phase yet.
detail.noSurveysDescription: Create your first survey to start collecting responses.
detail.completedBanner: Research complete. Reopen to make changes.
detail.trashedBanner: In trash. Will be permanently deleted on {date}.
detail.contextBadgeDescription: A project groups related surveys and insights around a single problem or idea you want to validate. It tracks your research progress from initial hypothesis through data collection to a final verdict.
detail.removeImageConfirmDescription: The image will be permanently deleted.
detail.imageUploadFailed: Failed to upload image. Please try again.
detail.imageInvalidType: Only JPEG, PNG, and WebP images are supported.

about.title: About
about.placeholder: Describe what this project is about…
about.discardDescription: You have unsaved changes. Are you sure you want to discard them?

notes.selectNoteDescription: Choose a note from the sidebar to start editing.
notes.confirmDeleteFolderDescription: Notes in this folder will be moved to Unfiled.
notes.confirmPermanentDeleteDescription: This note will be permanently deleted and cannot be recovered.
notes.confirmEmptyTrashDescription: All notes in trash will be permanently deleted.

phaseCards.title: Phase Progress
phaseCards.meta: {surveys, plural, one {# survey} other {# surveys}} · {responses, plural, one {# response} other {# responses}}

empty.title: No data yet
empty.description: Create your first survey to start seeing analytics and insights here.
empty.noSurveysDescription: Create a survey to start collecting responses from your target audience.
empty.noInsights: Signals will appear automatically as you collect survey responses.
empty.noInsightsDescription: Signals appear automatically from survey responses.

phases.ideaDescription: Defining the problem and hypothesis. Focus on understanding your target audience's pain points before jumping to solutions.
phases.researchDescription: Gathering data through surveys. Create surveys to test your hypothesis and collect evidence from potential users.
phases.validationDescription: Confirming findings with additional research. Cross-validate your earlier findings with a broader audience to build confidence.
phases.decisionDescription: Making go/no-go decisions based on evidence. Review all collected data to decide whether to proceed, pivot, or stop.
phases.label: Research Phase

findings.title: Findings
findings.empty: No findings yet. Findings appear automatically as survey responses come in.

suggestions.title: Suggested
suggestions.minResponsesBanner: Suggestions are generated automatically based on completed surveys.
suggestions.dismissDescription: This suggestion will be hidden. It won't appear again unless new data supports it.
suggestions.empty: Complete a survey to get automatic suggestions

noData.label: No Data
noData.summary: Start collecting responses to see how your idea stacks up.
noData.description: No responses have been collected yet. Once data starts coming in, the system will analyze it and provide a verdict on your idea's viability.

exploring.label: Exploring
exploring.summary: Early data is coming in — keep collecting responses to strengthen your confidence.
exploring.description: Early responses are being analyzed. The data is still inconclusive — keep collecting responses to build a stronger signal.

exploringLow.summary: You have some responses but not enough signals yet. Keep going!

promising.label: Promising
promising.summary: The data looks positive so far. Your idea is showing signs of product-market fit.
promising.description: Collected responses indicate positive interest in your idea. The data is trending towards validation, but more responses will strengthen confidence.

needsAttention.label: Needs Attention
needsAttention.summary: Some concerning signals have appeared. Review your findings to understand the risks.
needsAttention.description: The data reveals mixed or negative signals. Review your survey findings and consider adjusting your approach or hypothesis.

validated.label: Validated
validated.summary: Strong evidence supports this idea. The data consistently points to real demand.
validated.description: Strong, consistent evidence from responses supports your idea. The data indicates real demand and product-market fit potential.

invalidated.label: Invalidated
invalidated.summary: The data suggests this idea may not have enough demand. Consider pivoting.
invalidated.description: The collected data consistently indicates insufficient demand or interest. Consider pivoting your approach or exploring a different problem space.

moreData.summary: Collecting more responses will help paint a clearer picture.

overview.createFirstSurvey: Create your first survey to start collecting data
overview.noActivityDescription: Publish a survey to start seeing activity here.

keyFindings.empty: No findings yet. Collect survey responses to see key findings here.

nextStep.createSurvey: Create your first survey to start collecting data.
nextStep.activateSurvey: You have draft surveys ready. Activate one to start collecting responses.
nextStep.shareSurvey: Share your active survey to get more responses and strengthen your data.
nextStep.continue: Keep collecting responses to build even stronger confidence.

dangerZone.deleteConfirmDescription: This will permanently delete the project, all its surveys, questions, responses, and notes. This action cannot be undone.

landing.anonymousInfo: Your responses are anonymous. No login required.

completion.feedbackPlaceholder: Share anything else you'd like us to know...

thankYou.title: Thank you!
thankYou.description: Your responses have been recorded. Thank you for taking the time to share your thoughts.

closed.notFound: This survey could not be found. It may have been removed or the link may be incorrect.

closed.cancelled: This survey has been withdrawn by its creator.

errors.startFailed: Failed to start the survey. Please try again.
errors.saveFailed: Failed to save your answer. Please try again.
errors.submitFailed: Failed to submit your responses. Please try again.
errors.emptyResponse: Please answer at least one question before submitting.
errors.duplicateResponse: It looks like you've already completed this survey from this device.

sidebar.noOtherSurveys: No other surveys in this project

hero.title: Discover Real Problems Before You Build
hero.description: Pain Point Studio helps makers and teams validate ideas through structured customer research. Find out what people actually need in 15 minutes, not 3 months.

problems.title: The Problem Most Makers Face
problems.description: 70% of new products are abandoned within 3 months. Not because of bad execution, but because they solve problems that don't exist.

vacuum.title: Building in a Vacuum
vacuum.description: You spend 3 months building a product only to discover nobody wants it. Your problem isn't everyone's problem.

skills.title: No Research Skills
skills.description: You know you should talk to users, but you don't know what questions to ask or how to interpret the answers.

access.title: No Access to Users
access.description: Even with good questions, where do you find project managers or designers to interview? LinkedIn has 5% response rates.

howItWorks.title: From Idea to Validated Problem in 15 Minutes
howItWorks.description: Stop theorizing. Start collecting real data from real people.

research.title: Choose Your Research Area
research.description: Pick any topic you're curious about. "Productivity for ADHD" or "Freelancer Onboarding" or "Remote Team Management". No perfect idea needed.
research.exampleDescription: Exploring pain points around focus, task management, and workflow optimization for busy professionals.

questions.title: Use Proven Questions
questions.description: We provide research-backed question templates. No guessing. No leading questions. Just proven patterns that uncover real pain points.
questions.question1: Describe the last time you felt you lost control of your time at work. What happened?
questions.question2: What tools do you currently use? What frustrates you most about them?

share.title: Share & Collect Responses
share.description: Get a simple link. Share it anywhere - Twitter, LinkedIn, Reddit, Discord. Zero friction for respondents. No login required. 10 minutes to complete.

patterns.title: See Patterns Emerge
patterns.description: After 10-20 responses, patterns become obvious. Either you see a clear problem worth solving, or you don't. No more analysis paralysis.

functionalMinimalism.title: Built on Functional Minimalism
functionalMinimalism.description: Every feature must pass one test: Is it absolutely necessary to go from "I don't know what to build" to "I have a validated problem worth solving"?

onboarding.title: No Complicated Onboarding
onboarding.description: See "Start New Research". Click it. Fill form. Get link. Send to people. Collect answers. That's it. 15 minutes from signup to first feedback.

bloat.title: A Dashboard That Works for You
bloat.description: Your dashboard shows your research missions with real-time response tracking, completion metrics, and a timeline chart. Every element earns its place — no vanity widgets, no clutter.

solo.title: Solo First, Social Optional
solo.description: Use it privately forever if you want. All social features are opt-in. Your research is private by default. Share only when you choose to.

action.title: Action Over Analysis
action.description: Stop spending months theorizing. Pick an area. Ask questions. Get data. Decide. One week of real research beats one month of planning.

personas.title: Built for Different Types of Makers
personas.description: Whether you prefer working alone or finding collaborators, Pain Point Studio adapts to your style.

solo.title: Solo Steve
solo.description: Just wants to validate ideas quietly. No social pressure. No public profiles. Private research forever.

collaborative.title: Collaborative Chris
collaborative.description: Wants to find co-founders and team members. Share research publicly. Build together.

learning.title: Learning Laura
learning.description: New to product development. Needs templates and guidance. Wants to learn by doing.

features.title: Everything You Need, Nothing You Don't
features.description: Focused tools that help you validate ideas without the bloat.

templates.title: Research Templates
templates.description: Pre-built question sets for common research areas. Customer interviews, problem discovery, feature validation.

sharing.title: Zero-Friction Sharing
sharing.description: Simple link. No login required for respondents. Works on any device. 30-40% response rates vs industry 5-10%.

patterns.title: Pattern Recognition
patterns.description: Automatic highlighting of common themes across responses. See what problems appear repeatedly.

privacy.title: Privacy First
privacy.description: All research is private by default. Share publicly only when you choose. Full control over your data.

community.title: Community Access
community.description: Find other makers researching similar problems. Form teams. Share insights.

setup.title: Fast Setup
setup.description: From signup to collecting your first response in under 15 minutes. No tutorials needed.

cta.title: Stop Guessing. Start Knowing.
cta.description: Join makers who validate ideas before they build. Your first 5 research missions are free.

settings.title: Settings
settings.description: Manage your account settings and preferences.
